channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Man City consider legal action over Real candidate's promise to sign Haaland
item.description: After a candidate in Real Madrid's presidential election unveils an Erling Haaland shirt on live TV, Manchester City dismiss the suggestion their striker will move to Spain.

item.title: Brighton expect second Spurs bid for Van Hecke
item.description: Brighton are expecting Tottenham to return with an increased offer after rejecting an initial £70m bid for Netherlands defender Jan Paul van Hecke.

item.title: What is happening with World Cup ticket prices?
item.description: With falling prices, fluctuating availability and a lack of clarity, could the biggest World Cup ever see swathes of empty seats?

item.title: WSL has 'the edge' to attract Europe's best players
item.description: England defender Lucy Bronze says the Women's Super League has "the edge" of improving each year so is able to attract Europe's best players.

item.title: Guardiola quit '100 times' in the past - Khaldoon
item.description: Chairman Khaldoon Al Mubarak knew Pep Guardiola was serious about quitting as Manchester City manager after talking him round on previous occasions.

item.title: A higher ceiling than Messi? What next for Lamine Yamal?
item.description: Lamine Yamal is a key figure in Spain's World Cup squad, but why is the 18-year-old quick to dismiss Messi comparisons?

item.title: Heat experts warn 'reckless' Fifa of bottle ban health risk
item.description: Fans will not be allowed to take reusable water bottles into World Cup stadiums due to safety concerns, Fifa says in a late policy change.

item.title: Former Scotland forward Brazil 'lucky' to be alive
item.description: Former Scotland striker Alan Brazil says he is "very, very lucky to be here" after recent liver transplant.

item.title: Heroes and villains - the stories behind Scotland's World Cup bogeymen
item.description: Since their first attempt in 1954 there's always been some kind of misfortune lurking in the shadows for Scotland at World Cups, writes Tom English.

item.title: EFL scraps three-window transfer ban for late payments
item.description: Clubs in the English Football League vote to reduce the length of free-restriction transfer windows for late payments.

item.title: Two goals in 41 games - what next for Delap at Chelsea?
item.description: A year after striker Liam Delap joined Chelsea, he struggled to prove himself during an underwhelming and injury-hit season.

item.title: 'I need to put myself first' - McCabe on Chelsea move
item.description: Republic of Ireland captain Katie McCabe says that she had to "put myself first" as she opens up about her switch from Arsenal to Chelsea.

item.title: Wembley defeat, another manager sacked - what's going on at Salford City?
item.description: Famous owners but stuck in League Two with another manager sacked - what's happening at Salford City?

item.title: Wilkinson spends night in airport but says Wales will be ready
item.description: Head coach Rhian Wilkinson says Wales cannot let travel chaos affect their performance in Friday's Women's World Cup qualifier in Montenegro.

item.title: Video vault and Leeds litter picking - inside the mind of Bielsa
item.description: It is possible that nobody alive today has watched more football than Marcelo Bielsa, the former Leeds United boss who will lead Uruguay at the World Cup.

item.title: Toone learned 'massive lesson' from hip injury setback
item.description: Ella Toone says she learned a "massive lesson" from her recent injury setback and now she is ready to perform again for England.

item.title: Shearer on Beckham, pressure and Argentina heartbreak
item.description: Alan Shearer reflects on his and England's performances at the 1998 World Cup.

item.title: Diana Ross & 'GOAT' Ronaldo - Richards watches World Cup moments
item.description: Former England defender and current pundit Micah Richards looks at some of the most memorable World Cup moments.

item.title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo - watch the trailer
item.description: The story of how Lionel Messi v Cristiano Ronaldo became the rivalry that transformed football and defined a generation.

item.title: Liverpool will be enormous step for Iraola - Sutton
item.description: The Monday Night Club discuss Arne Slot's sacking from Liverpool and whether Andoni Iraola is the right replacement.

item.title: Like father like son - international goals run in the family
item.description: After Lewis Koumas followed in father Jason's footsteps to score for Wales, BBC Sport showcases the famous father-son footballers who have scored goals at international level.

item.title: Chelsea legend Tambling dies aged 84
item.description: Chelsea's former record goalscorer Bobby Tambling - who netted 202 goals for the club - dies at the age of 84.

item.title: Who are the World Cup's record holders?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the record holders in World Cup history ahead of the 2026 tournament

item.title: York City accept Hearts offer for Fagan-Walcott
item.description: York City accept what would be a club-record, seven-figure bid for defender Malachi Fagan-Walcott from Heart of Midlothian.

item.title: AC Milan chase Summerville - Thursday's gossip
item.description: AC Milan rival Premier League sides for West Ham's Crysencio Summerville, Real Madrid to sign Inter's Denzel Dumfries and Newcastle target Nigerian teenager Zadok Yohanna.

item.title: Scott poised to make England debut in World Cup friendlies
item.description: Bournemouth midfielder Alex Scott is poised to make his England debut in the forthcoming World Cup warm-up games in the United States.

item.title: Argentina fans arrive after cycling to Kansas City
item.description: Three friends from Argentina arrive in Missouri in Kansas after cycling 10,500 miles from their home in Gualeguaychu to the venue of their team's opening game of the World Cup.

item.title: Tuchel tackles questions from team of under-11s
item.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel fields questions from under-11 players before his team head to the World Cup.

item.title: Watch as Celtic beat Rangers in Women's Scottish Cup final
item.description: Watch as a Morgan Cross goal is enough to give Celtic victory over Rangers in the Women's Scottish Cup final despite a red card for Emma Lawton.

item.title: Milner a remarkable person, says Brighton chief exec
item.description: Brighton chief executive Paul Barber calls James Milner a remarkable person - and says they tried to persuade the midfielder to postpone his retirement and stay on for another year.

item.title: Watch Scotland put four past Curacao in World Cup send-off
item.description: Watch Scotland come from behind to beat 10-man Curacao in their World Cup send-off at Hampden.

item.title: How Slot went from champion to sack in a year
item.description: Just one year after lifting Liverpool's 20th league title in his debut season, Arne Slot has been sacked. BBC Sport looks back on how a dream start unravelled on Merseyside.

item.title: The Sunderland footballer setting record straight after 46 years
item.description: When Roly Gregoire quit football in 1980 after suffering a knee injury, he had faced so much racism that he could not bear to watch football for many years.

item.title: Spurs secure Premier League survival with win over Everton
item.description: Joao Palhinha's goal just before half time helps Tottenham overcome Everton to secure the result they needed to ensure Premier League survival on a day of nerve-shredding tension.

item.title: Most experience? Youngest? Best leagues? World Cup squads in numbers
item.description: BBC Sport look at all of the World Cup 2026 squads and answer some of the key questions.

item.title: Iraola will make Liverpool fans excited again - Murphy
item.description: Andoni Iraola is set to be announced as the Liverpool head coach this week - but what is the feeling around the city to his impending arrival?

item.title: Miller hopes to make a name for himself at Hearts
item.description: Calvin Miller agrees a three-year contract to join Hearts from Falkirk later this month.

item.title: The anatomy of a Golden Boot winner - and who could win it in 2026?
item.description: A look at the common attributes of World Cup Golden Boot winners and the claims of the leading 2026 candidates examined.

item.title: Is spying as common in Germany as Southampton boss Eckert claims?
item.description: BBC Sport explores whether spying is as regular an occurrence in Germany as Southampton boss Tonda Eckert claims.

item.title: Back shaving & biggest posers - the Scotland squad in their own words
item.description: John McGinn packed an European plug adaptor. Lyndon Dykes follows Ryan Christie around like a dog. Liam Kelly shaves Grant Hanley's back. Often.

item.title: Who needs what in World Cup qualifying?
item.description: England will seal a place at next summer's World Cup if they avoid defeat against Spain on Friday, but who else can secure a spot in Brazil?

item.title: World Cup 2026: How to follow on the BBC
item.description: The biggest World Cup in history starts on 11 June and there are more ways to follow it on the BBC than ever before.

item.title: Why do managers struggle after stepping up to 'big-six' clubs?
item.description: Will Liverpool-bound Andoni Iraola buck the trend of Premier League managers struggling after moving to a so-called 'big-six' club?

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: Download your World Cup 2026 wallchart
item.description: Get your essential piece of kit now by downloading the Fifa World Cup 2026 wallchart.

item.title: World Cup 2026: All 48 squads confirmed
item.description: All 48 confirmed squads for the 2026 World Cup in the US, Canada and Mexico.

item.title: The greatest World Cup shirts - and what makes them iconic
item.description: What are the best ever World Cup shirts and what makes them iconic? BBC Sport ranks the all-time top 10 here.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What are the deadlines for World Cup squads?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the deadlines by which teams must name their final squads for the 2026 World Cup

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Which club received most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the teams who have received the most red cards in a Premier League season.

item.title: Who won the Premier League golden boot?
item.description: BBC's Ask Me Anything team explains who has won the Premier League golden boot and other individual awards.

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: Unsold Scotland World Cup tickets put on resale sites
item.description: Dozens of tickets for the Scotland vs Haiti match on 12 June are listed on the third-party sites.

item.title: Celtic hold talks with Keane, plan discussions with O'Neill - gossip
item.description: Talks continue over future of Celtic manager post as Hibernian midfielder is linked with West Ham.

item.title: Aston Villa top scorer Hanson set to join Spurs
item.description: Aston Villa forward Kirsty Hanson is set to join Tottenham following an impressive season in the Women's Super League.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.
